name: Repeal of Corn Laws
notes: passed in 1846 but transitional duties ended in 1849

name: EFTA Accession
notes: only amongst member countries; our tau series has slight increase in tariffs after this; gradual phase in until 1966

name: Import Surtax
notes: revenue for Franco-Prussian war reparations; from Barnichon & Singh (2025)

name: Tariff on Iron Steel and Sugar
notes: protect domestic industry; from Barnichon & Singh (2025)

name: 1931 Tariff Revision
notes: also change in 1932; unsure about exogeneity (can take Smoot-Hawley exclusion approach?)

name: Zollverein Reform (early implementation)
notes: excluded: early deflator data too volatile

name: Franco-Prussian Commercial Treaty
notes: excluded: early deflator data too volatile

name: Post-Unification Tariff Reform
notes: very muddy because Cobden-Chevalier treaty at same time extended to Italy and liberalized

name: Dutch Liberal Tariff Reform
notes: tau was already at only 1% before change

name: 1921 Tariff Reform
notes: evidence also muddled; us govt document notes some increases but that they had very little effect (page 12 of doc): https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/SERIALSET-09125_00_00-020-0024-0000/pdf/SERIALSET-09125_00_00-020-0024-0000.pdf

name: Preferential Agreement with EC
notes: very small change, more of a pronounced drop immediately after GATT Kennedy Round

name: Tariff Autonomy Revision
notes: more about autonomy than liberalization; drop in tau afterwards

name: 1911 Tariff Revision
notes: more about autonomy than liberalization; drop in tau afterwards
